title: nicotiana occidentalis subsp. obliqua
intro: Nicotiana occidentalis subsp. obliqua, also known as the Western tobacco, is a plant species belonging to the nightshade family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The Western tobacco can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal use, often combined with other herbs to enhance its benefits.
